title: Asplenium annetii
intro: Asplenium annetii, also known as Anetii's maidenhair spleenwort, is a species of fern in the family Aspleniaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering various health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, as well as potential antioxidant and hepatoprotective properties. The bioactive constituents responsible for these properties include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which have been isolated and characterized in various studies. Anetii's maidenhair spleenwort is commonly used to prepare tea, infusions, and tinctures, which are used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including digestive and respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Asplenium annetii belongs to the family Aspleniaceae and is classified under the order Polypodiales. It is further grouped under the class Polypodiopsida and division Pteridophyta. Asplenium annetii is a fern species that falls under the phylum Pteridophyta and kingdom Plantae. The classification of Asplenium annetii is as follows: Asplenium annetii, Aspleniaceae, Polypodiales, Polypodiopsida, Pteridophyta, Plantae. Asplenium annetii is a member of the genus Asplenium, which consists of various other fern species.

The plant is also believed to have antioxidant properties, which may help to protect against cell damage and promote overall health.
